_comment: Same schema as ReolinkDemo/camera_config.json so configs are portable. Copy to camera_config.json (gitignored) and fill in IP + password.
_nano_defaults_comment: On a Jetson Nano, default to the sub stream. Main is 4096x2784 H.265 ~30fps which costs ~33MB per raw frame after decode and starves the 4GB shared RAM. Sub stream is typically 640x480 H.264 which closely matches YOLOv8n's 640px input.

inference._vehicle_classes_comment: COCO class IDs: 2=car, 3=motorcycle, 5=bus, 7=truck. Default is cars only; the engine still scores all 80 classes (no inference cost saving) but we discard non-car detections, which also eliminates cross-class false positives.

output._html_idle_seconds_comment: HTML summary is regenerated when no tracks have been active for this many seconds AND at least one new track has finalized since the last regen. JSONL event log is appended on every finalization regardless.

http._comment: Built-in dashboard server. The Nano serves the session output directory over HTTP so any device on your LAN can open http://<nano-ip>:<port>/. enabled=false (or --no-http on the CLI) disables it.

snapshot._comment: Main-stream HTTP snapshot fetcher. When a tracked target's bbox crosses area_threshold_frac of the sub-stream frame area, an async HTTP request to /cgi-bin/api.cgi?cmd=Snap fetches a full-resolution still (typically 4K on RLC-1224A) for inspection / ALPR. Re-fires every time bbox area grows by refire_growth_factor since the last fire, up to max_snaps_per_track total fires (each saved as <prefix>_<id>_main_<N>.jpg, prefix = 'person' or 'vehicle' per class). Linked from the dashboard via a 4K badge. Disabled automatically when --video is used. keep_days > 0 enables a background cleanup that deletes any *_main_*.jpg older than that many days, sweeping all sessions under output/ every cleanup_interval_s seconds; set keep_days=0 to disable cleanup.
snapshot._min_sharpness_comment: Blur gate (opt-in, 0 = disabled). Minimum variance-of-Laplacian on the sub-stream bbox crop (downsampled to 128px) required to fire a main-stream HTTP snap. Skipped fires do NOT consume a snap slot, so the track keeps re-checking until either the blur clears or its bbox area drops back below threshold. Typical values at 896x512 sub-stream: sharp daylight ~100-400+, slight blur ~30-80, heavy motion blur <20. Useful around dusk when the camera's auto-exposure drags out the shutter and moving vehicles smear. Start around 50 and tune from the [snap] track N blur skip log lines.
